data.title: The SOL spot ETF saw net inflows of $9.44 million last week.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, the SOL spot ETF saw a net inflow of $9.44 million last week (April 20th to April 24th, Eastern Time). The SOL spot ETF with the largest net inflow last week was the Bitwise ETF BSOL, with a weekly net inflow of $6.2 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $825 million. The second largest was the Fidelity ETF FSOL, with a weekly net inflow of $2.8 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $158 million. As of press time, the total net asset value of the SOL spot ETF was $883 million, with an ETF net asset value ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of total SOL market capitalization) of 1.77%, and a historical cumulative net inflow of $1.02 billion.

data.title: Ethereum spot ETFs saw net inflows of $155 million last week, marking the third consecutive week of net inflows.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Ethereum spot ETFs saw a net inflow of $155 million last week (April 20th to April 24th, Eastern Time). The BlackRock ETF ETHA saw the largest net inflow last week, with a weekly net inflow of $138 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $11.97 billion. The BlackRock ETF ETHB followed, with a weekly net inflow of $60.9053 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $456 million. The Grayscale Ethereum Trust ETHE saw the largest net outflow last week, with a weekly net outflow of $49.2393 million, bringing its historical total net outflow to $5.25 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of the Ethereum spot ETF is $13.79 billion, the ETF net asset ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Ethereum's total market capitalization) is 4.91%, and the historical cumulative net inflow has reached $12 billion.

data.title: Bitcoin spot ETFs saw net inflows of $824 million last week, marking the fourth consecutive week of net inflows.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a net inflow of $824 million last week (April 20th to April 24th, Eastern Time). The BlackRock ETF IBIT saw the largest net inflow last week, with a weekly net inflow of $733 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $65.37 billion. This was followed by the Ark & 21Shares ETF ARKB, with a weekly net inflow of $59.6 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $1.61 billion. The Bitcoin spot ETF with the largest net outflow last week was the Grayscale Bitcoin Trust GBTC, with a weekly net outflow of $59.01 million, bringing its historical total net outflow to $26.22 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Bitcoin spot ETFs is $102.64 billion, the ETF net asset ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Bitcoin's total market capitalization) is 6.60%, and the historical cumulative net inflow has reached $58.23 billion.

data.title: XRP spot ETF saw net inflows of $15.74 million last week.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, XRP spot ETFs saw a net inflow of $15.74 million last week (April 20th to April 24th, Eastern Time). The XRP spot ETF with the largest net inflow last week was the Bitwise ETF XRP, with a weekly net inflow of $8.86 million, bringing XRP's historical total net inflow to $426 million. The second largest was the Franklin ETF XRPZ, with a weekly net inflow of $4.66 million, bringing XRPZ's historical total net inflow to $349 million. As of press time, the total net asset value of XRP spot ETFs is $1.1 billion, the ETF net asset value ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of XRP's total market capitalization) is 1.23%, and the historical cumulative net inflow has reached $1.29 billion.

data.title: AI Sector Growth Diverts Funds and Talent, Future Investments to Focus on Stablecoins and Financial Infrastructure
data.description: The rapid expansion of the AI sector has attracted significant investment and entrepreneurial talent, according to PANews. This shift is expected to influence future funding priorities, directing capital towards areas with clear business models such as stablecoins, payment systems, tokenization, real-world assets, and financial infrastructure. The boundaries between crypto venture capital and traditional venture capital are anticipated to merge more quickly as a result of these developments.

data.title: Suspicious APE Insider Trading Linked Address Profits from LDO
data.description: Suspicious insider trading linked to APE has reportedly resulted in significant profits from LDO, according to Odaily. Monitoring by Lookonchain reveals that the associated address has accumulated an estimated profit of $300,000. One wallet has closed its LDO long position, earning approximately $50,000, while another wallet continues to hold 5.96 million LDO long positions, valued at around $2.65 million, with a current unrealized profit of about $250,000.

data.title: A suspected APE insider has made a floating profit of approximately $300,000 on long LDO positions.
data.description: According to Lookonchain's monitoring, the addresses suspected of being linked to APE insider trading have accumulated a floating profit of approximately $300,000 on their long positions in LDO. One linked wallet has already closed its LDO long positions, realizing a profit of approximately $50,000; another wallet still holds 5.96 million LDO long positions, with a position value of approximately $2.65 million, currently showing a floating profit of approximately $250,000.

data.title: Telegram launches no-code AI bot building tool
data.description: Telegram has launched a no-code AI bot building tool. Users can obtain tokens through LobsterFather and connect to platforms such as Telewer, GPTBots, and Lazy AI to create bots, supporting models like GPT and Llama. The tool allows for configuring conversational styles, task distribution, and multi-bot collaboration, and is already integrated with Telegram Business for use in scenarios such as automated replies, community management, and customer inquiries. (Techiexpert)

data.title: Hubei police cracked their first cryptocurrency theft case, involving over 100 million yuan.
data.description: Guo Tingyu, a police officer from the Cyber ​​Security Brigade of the Qingshan Branch of the Wuhan Public Security Bureau, participated in solving Hubei's first cryptocurrency theft case. By tracing the fund flow and analyzing fake wallet code, he identified and dismantled a cryptocurrency theft gang involved in over 100 million yuan in illicit funds. All five suspects have been apprehended. The report states that the gang developed a fake cryptocurrency wallet app to lure users into downloading it, resulting in tens of thousands of downloads. Guo Tingyu has also participated in solving multiple other cryptocurrency-related criminal cases. (Hubei Daily)

data.title: Momentum Token Unlock Scheduled for May 4
data.description: Momentum (MMT) is set to unlock approximately 12.53 million tokens on May 4 at 10:00 AM UTC+8. According to ChainCatcher, this token release is valued at around $1.72 million. The data comes from Web3 asset data platform RootData, which tracks token unlock schedules.

data.title: Jupiter Lend raises JLP/JupUSD lending limit to $40 million
data.description: Jupiter Lend announced that it will increase the borrowing limit for JLP/JupUSD from $25 million to $40 million, adding $15 million in capacity. The product also supports a maximum loan-to-value (LTV) ratio of 85%, with a current maximum annualized yield of 33.4%.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Chinese Gold Jewelry Prices Remain Stable
data.description: On April 27, Jin10 reported that the prices of gold jewelry in China have remained largely unchanged compared to the previous day. Most brands are quoting prices within the range of 1,440 yuan per gram to 1,445 yuan per gram.

data.title: Jupiter Lend Increases JLP/JupUSD Lending Cap to $40 Million
data.description: Jupiter Lend has announced an increase in the lending cap for JLP/JupUSD from $25 million to $40 million. According to Foresight News, users can now borrow up to 85% loan-to-value (LTV) or engage in looping operations with JLP.

data.title: Polysights Analyzes Volatility in Prediction Markets
data.description:
Polysights, a third-party prediction market data platform, shared insights on X platform regarding volatility rankings based on an analysis of over 20,000 Polymarket markets over the past six months. According to Odaily, the findings reveal that cryptocurrency-related markets occupy four of the top ten positions, indicating significant information asymmetry and challenges in accurate predictions.

The analysis highlights deadline anxiety, where questions about whether an event will occur by a specific date tend to be more volatile than those asking who will win. Market outcomes often lean towards 'yes'—in markets exceeding $1 million, the probability of a 'yes' outcome is 50%, compared to an overall probability of 27%. When markets experience significant volatility, the final result is frequently 'yes'.

Medium-sized markets, ranging from $1 million to $7 million, are identified as chaotic zones, while markets exceeding $10 million show smoother price trends due to substantial capital involvement.

data.title: IMF Economist Warns of Increased Financial Crisis Risk Due to Deregulation
data.description:
Kenneth Rogoff, former chief economist at the International Monetary Fund (IMF), has issued a warning regarding the U.S. government's push for financial deregulation. According to ChainCatcher, Rogoff highlighted that the relaxation of regulations, particularly concerning bank capital requirements and regulatory transparency, is significantly increasing the risk of a systemic financial crisis in the United States.

Rogoff noted that one of the main reasons for supporting deregulation is to enable traditional banks to remain innovative and competitive in the face of cryptocurrency challenges, especially stablecoins pegged to the U.S. dollar. He cautioned that if cryptocurrency regulations are simultaneously relaxed, combined with deregulation in the traditional financial system, it could lead to a 'double deregulation' risk, potentially resulting in systemic collapse. Although a full-blown banking crisis may not occur in the short term, the risk has notably increased, with the regulatory balance between stablecoins and the traditional banking system becoming a critical concern.

data.title: Japan's Inflation Outlook Complicates BOJ Rate Decision, ING Says
data.description:
Japan's March CPI exceeded expectations, and inflation is expected to accelerate further, complicating the Bank of Japan's rate decision on Tuesday, according to Jin10. The market generally anticipates no change, but a rate hike remains possible. Government subsidies on energy and social welfare programs have lowered prices for gasoline, utilities, and education. Excluding these factors, inflation is already significantly above 2%, and this trend is expected to become clearer in the coming months, with April's CPI projected to rise to 1.7% year-on-year. From May, both overall and core inflation are expected to exceed 2%.

This year's 'Shunto' wage negotiations resulted in over 5% wage growth, with noticeable increases in small and medium-sized enterprises. Amid a weak yen and rising global energy prices, companies are expected to pass on increased input costs to consumers. These factors significantly complicate Tuesday's rate decision. ING believes the impact of the energy shock on inflation is more persistent and pronounced, while its effect on growth is relatively minor. This difference is expected to be reflected in the quarterly outlook report released on the same day. The bank forecasts the BOJ will raise its inflation expectation for fiscal year 2026 from 1.9% to 2.4%, and for fiscal year 2027 from 2.0% to 2.2%, while the GDP growth forecast for fiscal year 2026 is slightly lowered from 1.0% to 0.7%.

data.title: Bitcoin Shows Bullish Signals Amid Token Unlocking Events
data.description: Bitcoin is exhibiting two historical bullish signals, according to PANews. Analysts from VanEck have identified a negative funding rate and a decline in concentrated hash power as indicators of potential positive movement for the cryptocurrency. Additionally, tokens such as SUI are approaching significant unlocking events, drawing attention from several large investors. These developments are causing increased interest and activity in the market.

data.title: Base Ecosystem Token REPPO Surges to New Highs
data.description:
The Base ecosystem token REPPO has experienced a significant increase, surpassing $32 million. According to BlockBeats On-chain Detection, the token's market value is now reported at $29.2 million, marking a 32% rise over the past 24 hours, with a trading volume of $620,000.

Previously, the token's market value began its upward trajectory from $10 million following a strategic funding commitment of $20 million from Bolts Capital to the Reppo Foundation.

BlockBeats cautions users that meme coin trading is highly volatile, often driven by market sentiment and speculative concepts, lacking intrinsic value or practical use cases. Investors are advised to be aware of the associated risks.

data.title: Study Reveals Small Percentage of Accounts Drive Prediction Market Price Discovery
data.description: Researchers from London Business School and Yale University have found that approximately 3% of accounts in prediction markets are responsible for the majority of price discovery. According to NS3.AI, the study indicates that these accounts are more adept at forecasting outcomes and reacting to news. In contrast, the majority of participants contribute to trading volume with minimal informational value.

data.title: Polymarket Market Report: Top 10 Most Volatile Markets Out of Over 20,000 Markets, Cryptocurrency Events Account for 40%
data.description: Polysights, a third-party prediction market data platform, published an article on its X platform analyzing over 20,000 Polymarket markets over the past six months and providing volatility rankings. Key findings include: - Cryptocurrency-related markets occupy four of the top ten spots, indicating high information asymmetry and few truly accurate predictions. - Deadline anxiety is prevalent; questions involving "Will X occur before [date]?" generate more volatility than "Who will win?". - Market outcomes tend to be "yes"—the probability of a "yes" outcome is 50% in markets above $1 million, compared to only 27% overall; when the market experiences significant volatility, the final outcome is more often "yes." - Mid-sized markets ($1 million to $7 million) are in a volatile range; markets above $10 million show more stable price movements due to large capital inflows.
